findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name StaticResource JAX-RS path parameter used directly in ClassLoader.getResource
 * @description The StaticResource uses JAX-RS @PathParam which maps URL path segments directly to resource loading
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/static-resource-classloader-injection
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 *       external/cwe/cwe-073
 */

import java

from Method m, MethodCall resourceCall, Annotation pathParamAnn
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasName("StaticResource") and
  resourceCall.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  (resourceCall.getMethod().hasName("getResource") or 
   resourceCall.getMethod().hasName("getResourceAsStream") or
   resourceCall.getMethod().hasName("findResource") or
   resourceCall.getMethod().hasName("findResources")) and
  (pathParamAnn.getType().hasName("PathParam") or
   pathParamAnn.getType().hasName("QueryParam") or
   pathParamAnn.getType().hasName("MatrixParam"))
select m, "StaticResource method $@ calls $@ on a ClassLoader/Thread - URL path parameters can control which classpath resource is loaded, enabling access to sensitive resources like WEB-INF/ or class files.", m, m.getName(), resourceCall, resourceCall.getMethod().getName()
findings.hypothesis: StaticResource JAX-RS resource uses path params to load arbitrary classpath resources without validation

message.text: StaticResource method [getPath](1) calls [getResourceAsStream](2) on a ClassLoader/Thread - URL path parameters can control which classpath resource is loaded, enabling access to sensitive resources like WEB-INF/ or class files.

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name StaticResource details - full method body analysis
 * @description Examine exactly what StaticResource does with the path parameter
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/static-resource-details
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java

// Find all methods in StaticResource along with what they call
from Method m, MethodCall mc
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasName("StaticResource") and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable() = m
select m, "StaticResource.$@ calls $@ at line $@", m, m.getName(), mc, mc.getMethod().getName(), mc, mc.getLocation().getStartLine().toString()
findings.hypothesis: Investigating what StaticResource does with path parameter

StaticResource.[getPath](1) calls [getClass](6) at line [90](7)

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name StaticResource - missing WEB-INF and sensitive directory protection
 * @description StaticResource exposes classpath resources without restricting access to sensitive paths
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/static-resource-no-sensitive-filter
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 *       external/cwe/cwe-073
 */

import java

// Check if StaticResource has any checks against WEB-INF, META-INF etc
from Method m
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasName("StaticResource") and
  m.getName() = "getPath" and
  // No check exists for WEB-INF or sensitive paths
  not exists(MethodCall mc |
    mc.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
    mc.getMethod().hasName("contains") and
    exists(StringLiteral sl |
      sl.getParent*() = mc.getAnArgument() and
      (sl.getValue().toLowerCase().matches("%web-inf%") or
       sl.getValue().toLowerCase().matches("%meta-inf%") or
       sl.getValue().toLowerCase().matches("%..%"))
    )
  ) and
  not exists(MethodCall mc |
    mc.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
    mc.getMethod().hasName("startsWith") and
    exists(StringLiteral sl |
      sl.getParent*() = mc.getAnArgument() and
      sl.getValue().toLowerCase().matches("%web-inf%")
    )
  ) and
  not exists(IfStmt ifStmt |
    ifStmt.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
    ifStmt.toString().toLowerCase().matches("%web-inf%")
  )
select m, "StaticResource.$@ loads classpath resources from URL path parameter without checking for sensitive directories (WEB-INF/, META-INF/, ../ traversal sequences). An attacker can access sensitive configuration files and class files.", m, m.getName()
findings.hypothesis: StaticResource lacks filtering for sensitive classpath paths like WEB-INF, META-INF, and ../ sequences

message.text: StaticResource.[getPath](1) loads classpath resources from URL path parameter without checking for sensitive directories (WEB-INF/, META-INF/, ../ traversal sequences). An attacker can access sensitive configuration files and class files.

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name FileDirContext path traversal - canonical path check bypass
 * @description FileDirContext serves web application files - check if canonicalization happens before containment check
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/filedir-context-path-traversal
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 *       external/cwe/cwe-706
 */

import java
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.DataFlow

// Find places where canonical path is checked with startsWith AFTER file construction
from MethodCall startsWithCall, MethodCall canonicalCall, MethodCall fileCall
where
  startsWithCall.getMethod().hasName("startsWith") and
  startsWithCall.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasName("FileDirContext") and
  canonicalCall.getEnclosingCallable() = startsWithCall.getEnclosingCallable() and
  (canonicalCall.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalPath") or 
   canonicalCall.getMethod().hasName("toRealPath") or
   canonicalCall.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalFile")) and
  fileCall.getEnclosingCallable() = startsWithCall.getEnclosingCallable()
select startsWithCall, "FileDirContext uses startsWith for path containment check - verify canonical path is computed BEFORE this check. Canonical call: $@", canonicalCall, canonicalCall.getMethod().getName()
findings.hypothesis: FileDirContext path containment check uses startsWith after improper canonicalization

message.text: FileDirContext uses startsWith for path containment check - verify canonical path is computed BEFORE this check. Canonical call: [toRealPath](1)
FileDirContext uses startsWith for path containment check - verify canonical path is computed BEFORE this check. Canonical call: [toRealPath](1)

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name FileDirContext - startsWith path containment order analysis
 * @description Check if the startsWith in FileDirContext validates a normalized path correctly
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/filedir-context-containment-order
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 *       external/cwe/cwe-706
 */

import java
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.DataFlow

// Find the validate method in FileDirContext and see if canonicalization is done before the startsWith check
from Method validateMethod, MethodCall startsWithCall, MethodCall fileConstructor
where
  validateMethod.getDeclaringType().hasName("FileDirContext") and
  startsWithCall.getEnclosingCallable() = validateMethod and
  startsWithCall.getMethod().hasName("startsWith") and
  fileConstructor.getEnclosingCallable() = validateMethod and
  (fileConstructor.getMethod().hasName("toRealPath") or
   fileConstructor.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalPath") or
   fileConstructor.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalFile"))
select validateMethod, "FileDirContext.$@ contains a startsWith containment check at line $@ with canonicalization at line $@ - ORDER MATTERS: if canonicalization happens after containment check it is bypassable with ../ sequences.", validateMethod, validateMethod.getName(), startsWithCall, startsWithCall.getLocation().getStartLine().toString(), fileConstructor, fileConstructor.getLocation().getStartLine().toString()
findings.hypothesis: FileDirContext canonicalization may happen after startsWith containment check

message.text:
FileDirContext.[validate](1) contains a startsWith containment check at line [934](2) with canonicalization at line [931](3) - ORDER MATTERS: if canonicalization happens after containment check it is bypassable with ../ sequences.
FileDirContext.[validate](1) contains a startsWith containment check at line [942](4) with canonicalization at line [931](3) - ORDER MATTERS: if canonicalization happens after containment check it is bypassable with ../ sequences.

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name LogFilesContentSource and ClientStubsContentSource path traversal details
 * @description Check if these Admin GUI servlets validate file paths before serving them
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/admingui-file-serving-details
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java

// Get method calls in these classes to understand validation
from Method m, MethodCall mc
where
  (m.getDeclaringType().hasName("LogFilesContentSource") or
   m.getDeclaringType().hasName("ClientStubsContentSource")) and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  m.getName() != "<clinit>"
select m, "[$@.$@] calls $@ at line $@",
  m.getDeclaringType(), m.getDeclaringType().getName(),
  m, m.getName(),
  mc, mc.getMethod().getName(),
  mc, mc.getLocation().getStartLine().toString()
findings.hypothesis: Admin GUI file-serving content sources may lack path validation

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name DirConfigSource path traversal via MicroProfile Config secrets directory
 * @description DirConfigSource reads config from a directory - check if path is sanitized
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/dir-config-source-path-traversal
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 *       external/cwe/cwe-073
 */

import java
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.DataFlow

// Look at how DirConfigSource resolves property names to files
from Method m, MethodCall fileAccess
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasName("DirConfigSource") and
  fileAccess.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  (
    fileAccess.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "File") or
    fileAccess.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.nio.file", "Path") or
    fileAccess.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.nio.file", "Paths") or
    (fileAccess.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalPath") or
     fileAccess.getMethod().hasName("toRealPath") or
     fileAccess.getMethod().hasName("startsWith") or
     fileAccess.getMethod().hasName("normalize") or
     fileAccess.getMethod().hasName("resolve"))
  )
select m, "DirConfigSource.$@ calls $@ - examining file access pattern for path traversal", m, m.getName(), fileAccess, fileAccess.getMethod().getName() + " at line " + fileAccess.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: DirConfigSource may allow path traversal via property name containing ../ sequences

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name WebDirContext and FileDirContext path access from HTTP request URI
 * @description Web resource serving path may allow access to protected directories
 * @kind path-problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/web-dir-context-path-traversal
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.TaintTracking
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.FlowSources

module WebDirConfig implements DataFlow::ConfigSig {
  predicate isSource(DataFlow::Node source) {
    source instanceof RemoteFlowSource
  }

  predicate isSink(DataFlow::Node sink) {
    exists(MethodCall mc |
      (mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasName("WebDirContext") or
       mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasName("FileDirContext") or
       mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasName("ProxyDirContext")) and
      (mc.getMethod().hasName("lookup") or
       mc.getMethod().hasName("list") or
       mc.getMethod().hasName("getAttributes") or
       mc.getMethod().hasName("bind")) and
      sink.asExpr() = mc.getAnArgument()
    )
    or
    exists(ClassInstanceExpr cie |
      (cie.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasName("WebDirContext") or
       cie.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasName("FileDirContext")) and
      cie.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "File") and
      sink.asExpr() = cie.getAnArgument()
    )
  }
}

module WebDirFlow = TaintTracking::Global<WebDirConfig>;
import WebDirFlow::PathGraph

from WebDirFlow::PathNode source, WebDirFlow::PathNode sink
where WebDirFlow::flowPath(source, sink)
select sink.getNode(), source, sink, "WebDirContext/FileDirContext: user-controlled input from $@ reaches directory context operation.", source.getNode(), "HTTP source"
findings.hypothesis: WebDirContext/FileDirContext allows path traversal via HTTP request URI

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Zip slip in ExpandWar and PayloadFilesManager
 * @description Zip archive extraction without canonical path check in deployment/payload handling
 * @kind path-problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/zip-slip-deployment
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.TaintTracking

module ZipSlipDeployConfig implements DataFlow::ConfigSig {
  predicate isSource(DataFlow::Node source) {
    exists(MethodCall mc |
      mc.getMethod().hasName("getName") and
      (mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.util.zip", "ZipEntry") or
       mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.util.jar", "JarEntry")) and
      source.asExpr() = mc
    )
  }

  predicate isSink(DataFlow::Node sink) {
    exists(ClassInstanceExpr cie |
      cie.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "File") and
      sink.asExpr() = cie.getAnArgument()
    )
    or
    exists(MethodCall mc |
      mc.getMethod().hasName("resolve") and
      mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().getASupertype*().hasQualifiedName("java.nio.file", "Path") and
      sink.asExpr() = mc.getAnArgument()
    )
  }

  predicate isBarrier(DataFlow::Node node) {
    // Path has proper canonicalization or contains check
    exists(MethodCall mc |
      (mc.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalPath") or 
       mc.getMethod().hasName("toRealPath") or
       mc.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalFile")) and
      node.asExpr() = mc
    )
    or
    // Check for ".." in zip entry name
    exists(MethodCall mc |
      mc.getMethod().hasName("contains") and
      mc.getAnArgument().(StringLiteral).getValue() = ".." and
      node.asExpr() = mc.getQualifier()
    )
    or
    exists(MethodCall mc |
      mc.getMethod().hasName("startsWith") and
      mc.getAnArgument().(StringLiteral).getValue().matches("%/%") and
      node.asExpr() = mc.getQualifier()
    )
  }
}

module ZipSlipDeployFlow = TaintTracking::Global<ZipSlipDeployConfig>;
import ZipSlipDeployFlow::PathGraph

from ZipSlipDeployFlow::PathNode source, ZipSlipDeployFlow::PathNode sink
where 
  ZipSlipDeployFlow::flowPath(source, sink) and
  (
    sink.getNode().getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasName("ExpandWar") or
    sink.getNode().getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasName("PayloadFilesManager") or
    sink.getNode().getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasName("ZipStorage") or
    sink.getNode().getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasName("ZipFile") or
    sink.getNode().getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasName("ModuleExploder") or
    sink.getNode().getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasName("ConnectorsUtil") or
    sink.getNode().getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasName("InstallRootBuilderUtil") or
    sink.getNode().getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasName("InstanceRootBuilderUtil")
  )
select sink.getNode(), source, sink, "Potential Zip Slip in $@: ZipEntry.getName() reaches file path construction without proper path traversal check.", sink.getNode().getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType(), sink.getNode().getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName(), source.getNode(), "ZipEntry name"
findings.hypothesis: Zip extraction in deployment infrastructure uses ZipEntry names without proper path validation

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Admin GUI servlet path traversal - LogFilesContentSource and ClientStubsContentSource
 * @description These servlets use request parameters to fetch files - check if paths are validated
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/admingui-file-traversal-no-validation
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 *       external/cwe/cwe-073
 */

import java
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.DataFlow

// Find if the request parameter is ever checked for ".." or validated against a base directory
from Method m, MethodCall getParamCall, MethodCall fileCall
where
  (m.getDeclaringType().hasName("LogFilesContentSource") or
   m.getDeclaringType().hasName("ClientStubsContentSource")) and
  getParamCall.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  getParamCall.getMethod().hasName("getParameter") and
  fileCall.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  (fileCall.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "File") or
   fileCall.getMethod().hasName("getAbsolutePath") or
   fileCall.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalPath")) and
  // Check if there's NO startsWith or contains check for ".."
  not exists(MethodCall checkCall |
    checkCall.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
    (checkCall.getMethod().hasName("startsWith") or
     checkCall.getMethod().hasName("contains") or
     checkCall.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalPath") or
     checkCall.getMethod().hasName("toRealPath")) and
    (checkCall.getQualifier().getType().(RefType).hasQualifiedName("java.lang", "String") or
     checkCall.getQualifier().getType().(RefType).hasQualifiedName("java.io", "File") or
     checkCall.getQualifier().getType().(RefType).hasQualifiedName("java.nio.file", "Path"))
  )
select m, "$@.$@: Uses request parameter $@ directly in file operation $@ without any path validation check (no startsWith, contains, getCanonicalPath). Admin GUI servlet is vulnerable to path traversal.",
  m.getDeclaringType(), m.getDeclaringType().getName(),
  m, m.getName(),
  getParamCall, "getParameter()",
  fileCall, fileCall.getMethod().getName()
findings.hypothesis: LogFilesContentSource and ClientStubsContentSource use request params in file access without any validation
